stories.title: Starmer to Rally European Allies at NATO Summit in Ankara
stories.summary: Outgoing PM Keir Starmer is stressing British commitment to defence spending at the NATO meeting in Ankara, countering Trump's criticisms of European allies' contributions.

stories.title: Andy Burnham May Keep Cabinet Details Secret Until He Enters No. 10
stories.summary: The PM-in-waiting's planned 'one and done' cabinet announcement has created fears in Whitehall of a political vacuum as the transition approaches.

stories.title: Trump Barely Knows Who Britain's Next Prime Minister Is
stories.summary: Little-known abroad, Andy Burnham faces questions about how he will handle Donald Trump and define a new era of US-UK relations.

topics.name: Parliament
topics.description: House of Commons, House of Lords, PMQs, debates, legislation

stories.title: National Security (State Threats) Bill Passes Commons with 394 Ayes
stories.summary: MPs voted 394-to-agree to Lords Amendment 1 of the National Security (State Threats) Bill on 6 July, progressing the legislation through Parliament.

stories.title: Commons Business: Key Debates Scheduled for 6–10 July
stories.summary: The week's business includes e-petition debates in Westminster Hall and consideration of several government bills, including the Taxation (Energy and Vehicles) Bill.

stories.title: Andy Burnham Set to Succeed Starmer — 'The Herd is Stampeding'
stories.summary: Harriet Harman told Sky News that Andy Burnham will become the next Labour PM as momentum against Keir Starmer has become unstoppable.

stories.title: Andy Burnham Opposes Second Scottish Independence Referendum
stories.summary: The Labour MP expected to succeed Sir Keir Starmer as PM has ruled out agreeing to a second independence vote, angering some Scottish Labour figures.

stories.title: Celtic Leaders Doubt Burnham's Devolution Drive Will Go Beyond Words
stories.summary: Leaders from Wales, Scotland and Northern Ireland are sceptical about Burnham's devolution commitments after he made missteps in his early pitches to the nations.

stories.title: Tougher Political Donation Rules Announced Amid Farage Funding Row
stories.summary: As the row over Nigel Farage's unregistered donations continues, the government has announced tougher rules on political donations, with Conservatives supporting stricter oversight.

stories.title: Kemi Badenoch Praised in US Press for 'Political Courage'
stories.summary: Conservative Party leader Kemi Badenoch is receiving attention in the American press for her principled approach to opposition politics.

stories.title: Lib Dems Call on Farage to 'Be Straight with the British People' Over Gifts
stories.summary: The Liberal Democrats joined calls for transparency from the Reform UK leader after it emerged benefits from an ally had not been declared to Parliament.

stories.title: Do the Liberal Democrats Need to Add a Bit of Excitement?
stories.summary: Sky's Sam Coates and Politico's Anne McElvoy discussed whether Ed Davey's party needs to develop bolder policy positions to attract new voters.

topics.name: Reform UK
topics.description: Nigel Farage, party news and announcements

stories.title: Farage Faces Parliamentary Investigation Over £5m Unregistered Crypto Donation
stories.summary: The parliamentary standards commissioner is investigating Nigel Farage after a £5m donation from crypto backer Christopher Harborne was not declared; Farage claims the scrutiny is a 'hit job' by the establishment.

stories.title: Fraudster George Cottrell Seen at Numerous Reform Events Despite 'No Formal Role'
stories.summary: Nigel Farage has been urged to clarify his 'dependence' on Cottrell, a convicted fraudster, who was also present with Farage at events in Abu Dhabi in December.

stories.title: Farage is on the Brink — But If He Goes, Labour Can't Rest Easy
stories.summary: Commentary suggests Reform's funding scandals could bring down its leader, but cautions that Labour should not be complacent about the political threat Reform represents.

stories.title: Scotland Could Freeze AI Datacentre Projects in Challenge to UK's AI Strategy
stories.summary: The Scottish government is considering a sweeping moratorium on new datacentre developments, putting a key plank of the UK's national AI strategy at risk.

stories.title: Scotland 'Risks Losing Billions' if AI Datacentre Ban Put in Place
stories.summary: Business leaders have slammed the SNP's backing for a temporary ban on AI datacentre developments, warning it could cost Scotland billions in investment.

stories.title: Scottish Labour MSP Defies Burnham Over IndyRef2
stories.summary: Scottish Labour MSP Paul Sweeney has broken ranks with Andy Burnham after the would-be PM blocked a second independence referendum, deepening tensions in the party.

topics.name: Cabinet Office
topics.description: Government reshuffles, senior appointments, cross-department policy

stories.title: Rycroft Review: Government Responds to Foreign Financial Influence Report
stories.summary: The government has published its response to the Rycroft Review, which made recommendations to strengthen UK safeguards against foreign financial influence in political and public life.

stories.title: Yvette Cooper Floated as Potential Chancellor Under Burnham Government
stories.summary: Foreign Secretary Yvette Cooper has been publicly linked to the role of Chancellor in a future Burnham Cabinet, as preparations for the leadership transition continue.

topics.name: Elections & By-elections
topics.description: Upcoming or recent election results, polls

stories.title: June 2026 Polling Averages: Labour, Reform, Conservatives
stories.summary: YouGov, Opinium and Survation published their most recent June 2026 polls showing vote share averages across nine polling companies — Reform continuing to perform strongly.

stories.title: Trump Calls Farage to Congratulate Reform on 'Recent Success'
stories.summary: In a phone call, President Trump congratulated Nigel Farage on Reform UK's political performance, believing the chaos in British politics plays to Farage's advantage.

stories.title: House of Lords: Second Reading of Armed Forces Bill
stories.summary: The Lords held a second reading debate on the Armed Forces Bill on 6 July, discussing proposed reforms to boost the size of the strategic reserve.

stories.title: Lords Amendments to National Security (State Threats) Bill Agreed by Commons
stories.summary: The House of Commons voted to agree to Lords Amendment 1 of the National Security (State Threats) Bill, advancing the legislation towards Royal Assent.
